channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Proposals to cut down on bad driving cross political lanes in Minnesota session
item.description: A variety of bills that aim to make Minnesota’s roads less treacherous are rolling their way through the legislative process. It could wind up as one area of bipartisan consensus in a session where that is a paramount.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Senate works to avert partial government shutdown ahead of midnight deadline
item.description: The Senate finds itself in a familiar position, working to avoid a partial government shutdown with just hours to spare.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended. Here's what borrowers should know
item.description: The Trump administration’s recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Cora Lewis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US transfers immigrants out of Guantanamo Bay to Louisiana as court weighs legality
item.description: Authorities have removed U.S. immigrants from detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay as a federal court in Washington weighs a challenge by civil rights advocates to holding immigrants at the offshore military station.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Two judges in mass firings cases order Trump administration to rehire probationary workers for now
item.description: Two federal judges have handed down orders requiring President Donald Trump’s administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Activist Mahmoud Khalil's arrest 'deeply concerning': Ex-NYC mayor
item.description: "You don't get arrested for disagreeing with the U.S. government," said former NYC Mayor Bill de Blasio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadians face stiffer rules for extended US stays
item.description: Canadians who stay in the U.S. for 30 days or longer face fines and charges for not registering as foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: A Republican-backed bill would upend voter registration. Here are 8 things to know
item.description: Congressional Republicans are pushing legislation that would make sweeping changes to voter registration, including requiring those signing up to present documents proving U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: House GOP passes spending bill, sending plan to avert government shutdown to Senate
item.description: The bill puts significant pressure on Democrats in the Senate, whose votes will be needed in order to avoid a shutdown. The federal government runs out of money at the end of the day on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh and Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Meteorologist-turned-lawmaker advocates for life-saving supplemental weather radar
item.description: DFL state Sen. Robert Kupec, who represents the region including Moorhead and Detroit Lakes, joined Cathy Wurzer on Tuesday’s Morning Edition to explain why he thinks the state should invest more than $3 million over the next two fiscal years in the project.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada sex trafficking law 'nearly a parking ticket' for johns; higher fines, more exposure proposed
item.description: Getting tough on the customers who pay for sex is the best way to attack human trafficking, according to a woman who told her story to lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
